landing.text:
Substack
Home

Activity

Profile
Create
Make money doing the work you believe in
Start your Substack
Learn more
For you
Aaron Parnas
13h
Subscribe
Wow!! I am so excited to share that our reporting over the past several weeks, whether on Epstein files, the Supreme Court, or more, has reached tens of millions of people across the country.
We are building something amazing. And, we are doing it together.
I know I ask a lot, but it's because I rely on you to keep it going. Share this. Subscribe to support my work:
aaronparnas.substack.com

You always own your intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments. With full editorial control and no gatekeepers, you can do the work you most believe in.

People can now read your Substack posts in other languages.
Today we’re going one step further, announcing that longer-form posts on Substack can now be translated from English into 15 different languages, and from 100 different languages into English. This means language no longer has to be a barrier for the work you publish.

Farrah @Substack

Recent changes
This is a running list of notable product changes happening at Substack. For other announcements and company posts, check out our
blog
.
Product Release
5 years ago
Today, we’re releasing a new and improved Subscribers dashboard that helps you understand and interact with your subscribers in important ways.

Read more here:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-better-analytics-for-your-subscribers
Product Release
5 years ago
We’ve heard from many writers that they’d like more flexibility to customize the appearance of their Substack. Starting today, you’ll see a few new options for theming your newsletter.

Read more here:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-publication-themes
Product Release
6 years ago
Today, we’re introducing new ways to discover writers on our homepage, and the beta version of Substack Reader, a place to keep up with all your newsletters.

The homepage revamp includes the introduction of lists that display the top newsletters in various categories, as well as a ‘Featured’ section to highlight writers who might not otherwise appear on those lists.

Check out the blog post for more:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-reader-homepage
Join our team
Founded in 2017, Substack is based in San Francisco and backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Y Combinator, Fifty Years, and other great investors. We're building a better business model for writing.
Want to know more? See our
jobs page
.

analysis.name: Substack
analysis.tagline: Make money doing the work you believe in
analysis.value_proposition: A publishing platform where writers own their content, mailing list, and subscriber payments while monetizing directly through paid subscriptions.
analysis.ideal_customer: Independent writers, journalists, podcasters, and video creators who want to monetize directly via paid subscriptions without gatekeepers

features.name: subscription cancellation

differentiators: Writers own their intellectual property, mailing list, and subscriber payments
Full editorial control with no gatekeepers
Free to start — no upfront costs, platform takes a cut of paid subscriptions
Built-in discovery and cross-promotion via Notes, recommendations, and the Substack app
Multi-format publishing (text, podcast, video, live video, chat) on one platform

recent_ships.what: Post translation into 15 languages from English, and from 100 languages into English
recent_ships.source: People can now read your Substack posts in other languages
HN: We're the founders of Substack, we just launched an iOS app

recent_ships.what: New and improved Subscribers dashboard with better analytics
recent_ships.source: a new and improved Subscribers dashboard that helps you understand and interact with your subscribers

recent_ships.what: Publication theming options for customizing newsletter appearance
recent_ships.source: new options for theming your newsletter

recent_ships.what: Substack Reader beta and revamped discovery homepage
recent_ships.source: new ways to discover writers on our homepage, and the beta version of Substack Reader

praised: Independent monetization model — writers keep IP, mailing list, and payments
Broad reach via cross-promotion (creator citing 'tens of millions of people')
Multi-format support (newsletter + notes + podcast + video + chat)
complaints: Editor bugs — 'Writing /etc/hosts breaks the Substack editor'
UI issues costly to users — 'Substack's UI and 1Password temporarily cost me $2k'
Platform tensions with distribution channels — 'Twitter won't allow retweeting tweets linking to Substack'
Slow organic growth for smaller creators — 'grew my Substack from 439 to 451 free subscribers in just 11 months'

free_tier.limits: Free to publish; Substack takes 10% of paid subscription revenue plus Stripe fees

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Publish free content; go paid anytime with 10% platform fee on paid subscriptions

pricing.notes: No traditional SaaS tiers — Substack is free to use for creators; revenue comes from a 10% cut of paid subscription revenue (plus Stripe processing fees ~2.9% + $0.30). Pricing page primarily surfaces product/use-case landing pages rather than tier tables.
